topic: In China employee termination: role-relay operating memo for payroll execution
primary_keyword: China employee termination payroll handoff controls

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Framework for termination legality and compensation exposure.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Supports obligations linked to termination-month closure and offboarding processing.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Supports component-level treatment documentation in final payroll handling.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Operational discipline and documentation quality are major risk controls.

consensus: Handoff quality across functions is a primary determinant of termination payroll outcomes.
Acceptance criteria at each relay step reduce late-cycle errors and disputes.
Escalation rules should trigger from handoff quality failures, not only employee complaints.
uncertainties: City-level interpretation may differ for close evidence scenarios.
Some treatment details require local confirmation before release.
English-language summaries may not include full local implementation nuance.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: Adopt a role-relay model with handoff acceptance checks and fail-fast escalation when transfer quality is below threshold.
